notes: PDP Toys' own slowstick (1991) · the kids'-synth company's music game · twelve club meetings that teach the 3-oscillator voice the entire Oneironautics catalog is scored with. Marc Ostrom moonlighted on it, uncredited, on contract — then built the studio's whole audio pipeline around the voice it teaches. The Rosetta stick.

shelf.label_subtitle: twelve club meetings · the instrument everything is made of
shelf.shelf_condition: loved to death · the label is sticker-over-sticker · a name in wobbly seven-year-old marker, gone over twice
shelf.prior_owner: Tem's grandfather bought it in 1991 for Tem's parent, age seven
shelf.provenance_note: The family's own cart, on the shelf beside Olaf's collection. The save file still holds a loop recorded by a seven-year-old in 1991 · four bars, a little wrong in the middle. Nobody in Vol 7 comments on it.
shelf.cover_blurb: Your Riffmaster wants to show you something! Join DOT-DOT, BEEBO, MARCIE and TODD for twelve meetings of the official RIFFMASTER MELODY CLUB. No wrong notes. (Some notes are jazz.)
back_of_case: · Call and echo · the club claps for you.
· MEETING 12: OPEN MIC. Four bars. Yours.
· Your loop becomes the title music. Forever.
Requires: a PDP Riffmaster, ears, one couch (couch not included).
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: PDP Toys' contract composer is uncredited. His initials are in the ROM header anyway: M.O. Three years later every Oneironautics game hums with the same three oscillators. The studio's sound is a children's toy taken seriously, which is the studio in one sentence.
shelf.meta_note: MARCIE's last line is the whole design: 'now every song you ever hear on this machine is partly yours.'

lore_tokens_conditional.riffmaster_open_mic_recorded: an OPEN MIC loop recorded to the save · Estuary 3's cabin radio can tune to it, no comment
